cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 19th-century policy by which industrialized European states, the United States, and Japan extended political, military, and economic control over less industrialized regions of Africa, Asia, and the Pacific

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Rudyard Kipling's 1899 poem that framed imperialism as a moral duty to 'civilize' non-European peoples, widely used to justify colonial rule and cultural assimilation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The misapplication of Darwin's theory of natural selection to human societies, used to argue that European domination of other peoples reflected a natural hierarchy of 'fit' races and nations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Territories fully settled and directly governed by an imperial power, with colonial administrators replacing or displacing local political authority

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A form of imperialism in which colonizers permanently migrate to occupy land, displace Indigenous peoples, and build a reproducing settler society — as in Australia, New Zealand, and the American West

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Nominally independent states whose foreign affairs and defense were controlled by an imperial power, allowing indirect rule without full annexation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A region in which a foreign power claimed exclusive trading, investment, or military privileges without formal annexation — commonly applied to European and Japanese zones in late Qing China

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1869 waterway across Egypt linking the Mediterranean and Red Seas that drastically shortened European shipping routes to Asia and became a strategic prize of British imperialism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A mosquito-borne tropical disease that long limited European penetration of sub-Saharan Africa until prophylactic treatments allowed deeper colonial expansion in the 19th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An anti-malarial drug extracted from cinchona bark that dramatically reduced European deaths in tropical climates and enabled the late-19th-century Scramble for Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The first fully automatic machine gun, invented in 1884, whose overwhelming firepower gave European armies a decisive edge over African and Asian resistance forces

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A political ideology emphasizing loyalty to a shared nation-state, language, or culture — both a motive for European empire-building and later a driver of anti-colonial independence movements

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An 1890 Plains Indian spiritual movement promising the return of ancestors and the retreat of settlers; its suppression culminated in the Wounded Knee Massacre

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The southeastern Indigenous nation that adopted a written constitution and syllabary but was forcibly removed from its homeland under the 1830 Indian Removal Act

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1838 forced march in which thousands of Cherokee died during relocation from the American Southeast to Indian Territory, embodying U.S. settler colonialism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An Andean messianic belief in the return of a beheaded Inca king whose body would reunite to restore Indigenous rule, inspiring resistance to Spanish and later criollo domination

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1857 uprising of Indian soldiers against the British East India Company, triggered by rumors of greased cartridges and leading Britain to impose direct Crown rule as the British Raj

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1896 Ethiopian victory over invading Italian forces under Emperor Menelik II — the most decisive African defeat of a European imperial army during the Scramble for Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Mid-19th-century conflicts in which Britain forced Qing China to legalize the opium trade, cede Hong Kong, and open treaty ports, exposing Chinese weakness to foreign encroachment

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A massive mid-19th-century civil war in Qing China led by Hong Xiuquan, who claimed to be Jesus' brother; its suppression killed tens of millions and further destabilized the dynasty

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A 1899–1901 anti-foreign, anti-Christian uprising in Qing China suppressed by an eight-nation foreign alliance, exposing the depth of imperial encroachment and hastening dynastic collapse

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1899 U.S.-proposed framework demanding equal trading access for all foreign powers in China, preventing the formal partition of Qing territory into exclusive colonies

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1900 Asante war against British rule, sparked when a governor demanded the sacred royal stool; led by Queen Mother Yaa Asantewaa in modern-day Ghana

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 19th-century nationalist movements in Greece, Serbia, Bulgaria, and Romania that broke away from Ottoman rule, weakening the empire and inflaming great-power rivalries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Late-19th-century Sudanese uprising led by Muhammad Ahmad, who declared himself the Mahdi and expelled Anglo-Egyptian forces before Britain reconquered Sudan at Omdurman in 1898

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: British mining magnate and prime minister of the Cape Colony whose De Beers diamond monopoly and vision of a Cape-to-Cairo railway epitomized European economic imperialism in Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: British author of 'The White Man's Burden' whose poetry popularized the civilizing-mission justification for imperialism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: British monarch (1837–1901) whose long reign saw the consolidation of the British Empire and who was proclaimed Empress of India in 1876

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Scottish missionary and explorer whose journeys into central Africa to map the Zambezi and oppose the slave trade fueled European interest in colonizing the continent

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American-born inventor of the first self-powered machine gun, whose weapon became emblematic of Europe's technological edge during the Scramble for Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: French diplomat and engineer who directed construction of the Suez Canal, linking European and Asian commerce and later attempted — unsuccessfully — to build the Panama Canal

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The late-19th-century partition of Africa among European powers, driven by industrial demand for raw materials and enabled by quinine, steamboats, and the Maxim gun

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1884–85 meeting at which European powers set rules for claiming African territory without African representation, formalizing the Scramble for Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 19th-century American belief that the United States was divinely destined to expand across North America, justifying war with Mexico and the displacement of Native nations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Theodore Roosevelt's 1904 extension of the Monroe Doctrine asserting U.S. authority to intervene in Latin America to preserve stability and protect U.S. interests
cards.answer: Roosevelt Corollary to the Monroe Doctrine

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A colonial governance style in which imperial officials replaced local leaders and imposed European laws, languages, and administration — practiced most notably by France

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Russian empress whose late-18th-century southern and eastern expansion laid the foundation for tsarist imperialism into the Caucasus, Crimea, and Central Asia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: U.S. president (1845–49) whose Manifest Destiny agenda provoked the Mexican-American War and added Texas, California, and the Oregon Country to the United States

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: U.S. president who issued the 1904 corollary asserting hemispheric policing powers and championed the 'big stick' approach to Latin American intervention

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Belgian monarch who personally owned the Congo Free State (1885–1908), where forced rubber collection killed millions and became a global byword for colonial atrocity

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The period of direct British Crown rule over the Indian subcontinent (1858–1947) imposed after the Sepoy Mutiny, replacing the East India Company administration

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Peruvian-Andean leader of a major 1780s uprising against Spanish colonial taxation and forced labor whose name was later adopted by 20th-century Indigenous resistance movements

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Indian sepoy whose 1857 refusal to use greased cartridges and attack on British officers helped spark the Sepoy Mutiny

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Ethiopian emperor who modernized his army and defeated the Italians at Adwa in 1896, preserving Ethiopian independence during the Scramble for Africa

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Chinese martial-arts society, practicing spiritual invulnerability rituals, whose 1899–1901 uprising against foreign missionaries and merchants became known in the West as the Boxer Rebellion

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Qing official whose 1839 seizure and destruction of British opium stocks in Canton triggered the First Opium War

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Chinese religious teacher who claimed to be Jesus' younger brother and led the mid-19th-century Taiping Rebellion against the Qing Dynasty

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Queen Mother of the Asante who led the 1900 War of the Golden Stool against British forces attempting to seize Asante sovereignty in modern-day Ghana

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Sudanese religious leader who proclaimed himself the Mahdi in 1881 and led a holy war that briefly expelled Anglo-Egyptian forces from Sudan

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Early-19th-century Zulu king whose military reforms built the Zulu Kingdom in southern Africa, later resisting Boer and British expansion

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Qing dowager empress who dominated late-19th-century China, initially opposing then tacitly supporting the Boxer Rebellion against foreign imperialism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Nitrogen-rich seabird droppings mined from Pacific coastal islands in the 19th century and exported to Europe and the U.S. as high-value fertilizer, enriching Peru's export economy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Charles Goodyear's process of treating natural rubber with sulfur and heat to make it durable and elastic, massively increasing industrial demand for Congolese and Amazonian rubber

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Non-food crops such as cotton, tea, rubber, and palm oil grown in colonies for export to industrial markets rather than local subsistence

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Colonial and semi-colonial economies organized around shipping a small range of raw materials to industrial powers, leaving producers vulnerable to global price swings

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A West African cash crop harvested from palm trees and exported for use in European industrial soaps, candles, and machine lubricants

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The agricultural practice of planting a single crop over vast colonial plantations — profitable for export but destructive to soil and food security

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The addictive narcotic British traders smuggled from India into Qing China to reverse a silver trade deficit, sparking the 19th-century wars that opened China to imperialism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1842 agreement ending the First Opium War that ceded Hong Kong to Britain, opened five treaty ports, and established the pattern of 'unequal treaties' imposed on Qing China

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Regions of China in which a foreign power claimed exclusive trade, investment, and railway-building privileges in the 1890s, effectively carving up the country without full annexation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Dutch colonial policy in Java requiring peasants to devote a fixed portion of land to export crops like sugar, coffee, and indigo for the colonial government

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A pejorative term for Central American and Caribbean states whose economies and politics were dominated by foreign fruit corporations like United Fruit, coined in O. Henry's Cabbages and Kings

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American short-story writer, better known by his pen name O. Henry, who coined the term 'Banana Republic' in his 1904 book Cabbages and Kings to describe U.S. fruit-company dominance in Honduras

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: To voluntarily leave one's country of origin to settle elsewhere — a driver of 19th-century mass movement from Europe and Asia to the Americas, Australia, and colonial territories

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A negative condition such as famine, war, or persecution that drives people to leave their homeland

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A positive condition such as job opportunities, available land, or political freedom that attracts migrants to a new destination

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The dispersion of a people from their original homeland — term applied to 19th-century communities of Indians, Chinese, Irish, and Africans scattered by labor migration and forced relocation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1845–49 potato blight in Ireland that killed roughly a million people and drove mass Irish emigration to the United States, Canada, and Australia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An overseas settlement used to house convicted criminals as a form of exile labor — notably the British shipment of prisoners to Australia beginning in 1788

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A system of enslavement in which people are legally classified as property and their status passes to their children — the dominant form of unfree labor in the Atlantic plantation economies

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Labor extracted through debt, contract, or state compulsion rather than full chattel enslavement — widely used in 19th-century plantations and colonial mines

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Labor arrangements in which workers technically consent but face heavy economic or legal pressure — such as sharecropping and debt peonage across post-abolition plantation economies

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A labor contract binding a worker to serve an employer for a fixed term — often 5–7 years — in exchange for passage; used extensively to move Indian and Chinese laborers to 19th-century colonial plantations

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Indian lawyer whose early career defending Indian indentured laborers in South Africa shaped his later nonviolent campaign against British imperialism in India

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Neighborhoods in which immigrants from the same region cluster together to share language, religion, and mutual aid — such as the Chinatowns and Little Italys of 19th-century port cities

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Mid-19th-century waves of migration — to California, Australia, South Africa, and the Yukon — drawn by mineral discoveries and transforming settler-colonial demographics

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Rail line completed in 1869 linking the U.S. East and West coasts, built largely by Chinese and Irish immigrant labor and accelerating settler displacement of Indigenous peoples

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A 19th-century recruitment system bringing South Indian indentured workers — overseen by a headman who managed advances and discipline — to tea and rubber plantations in Ceylon and Malaya

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1885 Canadian law imposing a head tax on Chinese immigrants to deter arrivals after completion of the Canadian Pacific Railway

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1882 U.S. law barring Chinese laborers from entering the country — the first federal statute to exclude an immigrant group by nationality

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Informal name for a bundle of early-20th-century immigration laws restricting non-European arrivals to Australia in order to maintain a British-descended majority

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Political organization founded in 1885 by Western-educated Indian elites to petition for greater Indian participation in British Raj governance — later the vehicle of the independence movement
